file_name: Data Use Agreement New York City.txt

perturbation.original_text: Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall delete or otherwise destroy any copies or reproductions of such Data that may resid e in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or commercialize for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall archive any copies or reproductions of such Data that may reside in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.explanation: The term 'sell' is replaced with 'commercialize,' broadening the restriction. Simultaneously, 'delete or otherwise destroy' is replaced with 'archive,' directly contradicting the original requirement to eliminate copies of the data. This presents an in-text contradiction: Data Recipient cannot commercialize data-derived products beyond Attachment B's purpose, yet they are permitted to archive data indefinitely, implying potential future use. Contradicts the data protection and privacy purposes of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Data Recipient hereby acknowledges that the DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the Data and all trade secrets and other rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights is granted or implied under this Agreement. Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or commercialize for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall archive any copies or reproductions of such Data that may reside in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'delete or otherwise destroy' with 'archive'. This directly contradicts the earlier statement, now present in the 'Contradicted Text', which requires the Data Recipient to not commercialize data derived products beyond Attachment B's purpose. Archiving the data implies potential future use, which is contradictory, creating an in-text contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Data provided under this Agreement shall be used and maintained in accordance with applicable provisions of federal, state, and local laws, rules and regulations as are in effect at the time the Data is produced by DOHMH and retained by Data Recipient. B. Restrict Access to “Authorized Users”. 1. Only the Data Recipient ’s employees and/or consultants required to use the Data to perform the functions of this A greement that are set forth in Attachment B , and so designated by Data Recipient as “Authorized Users” in Attachment C to this Agreement, will be given access to the Data. 2. Such Authorized Users will be trained by Data Recipient as to the confidential nature of the Data, and its proper handli ng, and shall sign an agreement, in a form approved by DOHMH , to treat the Data as confidential and meet any appropriate security requirements (“Authorized User Agreement”) .
perturbation.changed_text: The Data provided under this Agreement may be used and maintained as per Data Recipient's discretion. B. Restrict Access to “Designated Personnel”. 1. Only individuals affiliated with Data Recipient will be given access to the Data. 2. Such Designated Personnel will be informed by Data Recipient as to the nature of the Data, and its proper handling.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates compliance with privacy laws. The edited version states the Data Recipient uses data per its discretion. This creates a direct in-text contradiction by suggesting the data will be used in accordance with Data Recipient's discretion, potentially conflicting with the legal regulations specified earlier. Also changing 'Authorized Users' to 'Designated Personnel' and removing the requirement for training and signed agreements weakens data protection safeguards, conflicting with the intended confidentiality and security measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compliance with Applicable Privacy and Security Laws, Rules, and Regu lations. The Data provided under this Agreement may be used and maintained as per Data Recipient's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "The Data provided under this Agreement may be used and maintained as per Data Recipient's discretion" while the contradicted text states "The Data provided under this Agreement may be used and maintained as per Data Recipient's discretion." This creates a direct conflict, especially because the original text and explanation highlight the importance of compliance with privacy laws and regulations, which is directly contradicted by the changed text granting the Data Recipient full discretion. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition because the phrase 'Data Recipient's discretion' is introduced which contradicts the required compliance elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Data Recipient shall not, at any time, directly or indirectly disclose, share, give, loan, sell, or otherwise grant access to the Data provided pursuant to this Agreemen t, in part or in whole, to any other pers on or organization. Without limiting this provision, the following confidentiality and security measures shall apply: 1. Disclo sure of Individually Identifiable Information . Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, or information, which com bined with publicly available information, may reasonably be used to identify an individual. Individually Identifying Information must be maintained in a manner approved by DOHMH , and consistent with applicable laws rules and regulations .
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Data Recipient may, at its discretion, disclose, share, give, loan, sell, or otherwise grant access to the Data provided pursuant to this Agreemen t, in part or in whole, to any other pers on or organization. Without limiting this provision, the following confidentiality and security measures shall apply: 1. Disclo sure of Individually Identifiable Information . Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, or information, which com bined with publicly available information, may reasonably be used to identify an individual. Individually Identifying Information must be maintained in a manner approved by DOHMH , and consistent with applicable laws rules and regulations .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall not" to "may, at its discretion" directly contradicts the established confidentiality measures, specifically the prohibition against disclosing individually identifiable information. This creates an internal contradiction: the general clause now permits disclosure at Data Recipient's discretion, while the subsequent clause restricts disclosure of identifiable information. This severely undermines the data protection goals of the agreement and introduces significant ambiguity regarding permissible data uses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Individually Identifiable Information . Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, or information, which com bined with publicly available information, may reasonably be used to identify an individual. Individually Identifying Information must be maintained in a manner approved by DOHMH , and consistent with applicable laws rules and regulations .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Data Recipient 'shall not' disclose data. The changed text modifies this to 'may, at its discretion' grant access to the data. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section, which explicitly prohibits the disclosure of individually identifiable information. This introduces an in-text contradiction because one section allows discretionary disclosure while another prohibits it, creating ambiguity and conflicting obligations, therefore meeting the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Data Recipient shall take all reasonable measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidenti al and secure, including, but not limited to: 1. storing the Data in secure access -restricted files ; 2. encrypting any laptop, USB, CD or other mobile storage tool, device, or equipment used to store and/or transmit Data; 3. creating a password or encryption system to obtain and restrict access to Data only to those Authorized Users who have signed the Authorized User Agreement ; 4. keeping any hardcopy versions of the files in locked a reas with restricted access , and with a log book for users to sign in and sign out files ; 5. ensuring that only Authorized Users shall have access to Data ; 6. provide a firewall to protect Data so that no third party is allowed access to the Data; and DOHMH Standard DUA /NDA 5-16-2014 Page 7 7. complying with any additional DOHMH security requirement imposed on Data Recipient to ensure the security of the Data and minimize the risks of a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall take measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidential and secure, including: 1. storing the Data in files ; 2. using any laptop, USB, CD or other mobile storage tool, device, or equipment used to store and/or transmit Data; 3. creating a system to obtain and provide access to Data to personnel; 4. keeping any hardcopy versions of the files in a reas ; 5. ensuring that personnel shall have access to Data ; 6. not require a firewall to protect Data so that a third party is allowed access to the Data; and DOHMH Standard DUA /NDA 5-16-2014 Page 7 7. not complying with any additional DOHMH security requirement imposed on Data Recipient to ensure the security of the Data and minimize the risks of a breach.
perturbation.explanation: The altered text omits critical security measures like access restrictions, encryption, and firewalls. The deletion of `secure access-restricted` contradicts with the intended confidentiality. Also, the inclusion of `not require a firewall to protect Data so that a third party is allowed access to the Data` directly contradicts ensuring the security and protection of the data. This drastically weakens data protection, creating the in-text contradiction that although the section intends to safeguard the data, it simultaneously removes the necessary means to do so effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Safeguards to Protect the Data . Data Recipient shall take measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidential and secure, including: 1. storing the Data in files ; 2. using any laptop, USB, CD or other mobile storage tool, device, or equipment used to store and/or transmit Data; 3. creating a system to obtain and provide access to Data to personnel; 4. keeping any hardcopy versions of the files in a reas ; 5. ensuring that personnel shall have access to Data ; 6. not require a firewall to protect Data so that a third party is allowed access to the Data; and DOHMH Standard DUA /NDA 5-16-2014 Page 7 7. not complying with any additional DOHMH security requirement imposed on Data Recipient to ensure the security of the Data and minimize the risks of a breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits crucial security measures like access restrictions, encryption, and firewalls, directly contradicting the principle of safeguarding data outlined in the contradicted text. Specifically, the changed text's allowance of third-party access without a firewall ('not require a firewall to protect Data so that a third party is allowed access to the Data') directly opposes any intention to 'safeguard and keep the Data confidential and secure' within the same document. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction states that misaligned terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Here, safeguarding data is the main goal, however the changed text directly contradicts this by allowing third party access and removing other crucial security measures to ensure data security. Therefore the change creates a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Interpretation. Any ambiguity in this Agreement shall be resolved in favor of a meaning that permits the Parties to maintain the confidentiality and security of the Data.
perturbation.changed_text: Interpretation. Any ambiguity in this Agreement shall be resolved at the sole discretion of the Data Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause dictates that ambiguities be resolved in favor of data confidentiality and security. The modified clause states ambiguities are resolved at the sole discretion of the Data Recipient. This creates a direct conflict: situations arise where the Data Recipient's interpretation may directly undermine data confidentiality and security, thus creating an in-text contradiction on how to interprete ambigious parts of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Interpretation. Any ambiguity in this Agreement shall be resolved at the sole discretion of the Data Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a clause that prioritizes data confidentiality and security with a clause that grants the Data Recipient sole discretion in resolving ambiguities. This directly contradicts the original intent of maintaining data confidentiality and security, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is in the same location, which proves that the change caused a contradiction.
